beats.text: You just saw four rectangles and noticed differences between them. Things like how many rows they had or how long each row was. Now you're going to build your own rectangles on the grid, one row at a time. When you tap Check, you'll see how many squares you used. And we'll write a sentence that names what that area means. Everything we do today builds to one sentence: ___ is a multiple of ___ because ___ × ___ = ___. Let's build the first rectangle now.

params.description: Hundred Grid appears in Mode B. Grid-snap off, numeric cell labels hidden, side handles not exposed. Clean surface ready for free tile placement.
Tile bank appears with 12 tiles available for placement.

beats.text: You have 12 tiles. Place them on the grid to make a rectangle.

elements.description: Hundred Grid in Mode B. Grid-snap off, numeric labels hidden, side handles not exposed. Clean surface.
Tile bank with 12 tiles available.

beats.text: When your rectangle is complete, tap Check.
Place 12 tiles to make a rectangle, then tap Check.

validator.description: Student formed a valid rectangle with 12 tiles in rows of equal length

beats.text: Nice. Your rectangle has tiles in rows.

validator.description: Student placed tiles but did not form a valid rectangle on first attempt

beats.text: Not quite. A rectangle has rows of equal length.

validator.description: Student placed tiles but did not form a valid rectangle on second attempt

params.description: Grid shows example rectangle outline with 3 rows of 4 tiles each.

beats.text: Look at this example: 3 rows, 4 tiles in each row. Every row has the same number. Now use your 12 tiles to make rows that match.

validator.description: Student placed tiles but did not form a valid rectangle. System models a valid arrangement.

params.description: System arranges 12 tiles into 3 rows of 4, demonstrating equal row lengths.

beats.text: Let me show you. I'm placing 12 tiles in 3 rows. Each row has 4 tiles. Count them: row 1 has 4, row 2 has 4, row 3 has 4. When every row has the same number, that's a rectangle. The answer is 3 rows of 4.

elements.description: Hundred Grid in Mode B with 12 tiles placed in rectangular arrangement. Rows of equal length formed.
Tile bank now empty after all 12 tiles placed.

beats.text: Now fill in the sentence to describe what you built.

params.template: My rectangle has ___ tiles in ___ rows.

beats.text: Fill in: My rectangle has ___ tiles in ___ rows.

beats.text: That's it. You built rows of equal length. That describes a rectangle.

beats.text: Check your numbers. Count the total tiles, then count the rows.

validator.description: Student filled slots incorrectly on second attempt

params.description: One row of the rectangle highlights.

beats.text: The first blank needs the total number of tiles you placed. The second blank needs how many rows you made. Count the rows on your rectangle now.

validator.description: Student filled slots incorrectly. System models the correct completion.

params.description: System fills both slots with values matching the student's built rectangle.

beats.text: Here's how to fill it. I count all the tiles: that's 12 total. I count the rows you made: that's the row count. The sentence is: My rectangle has 12 tiles in your-row-count rows. The total goes first, then the row count. That describes what you built.

params.description: Rows in the rectangle highlight sequentially.

beats.text: Let's break this down. First count all the tiles. Then count the rows. Then count how many tiles are in one row. The area equals the total tiles.

validator.description: Student filled one or more slots incorrectly after multiple attempts. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System animates counting all tiles, then highlights rows sequentially, then shows one row's tile count, then displays area label.

beats.text: Here's how to read this rectangle. Count all the tiles to get the total. Count the rows by looking at each horizontal line. Pick one row and count its tiles to find the row size. The area equals the total number of tiles because area measures how many square units fill the shape. Now fill in the sentence using those four values.

beats.text: Right. Your sentence says the area is a multiple of the fixed side length because width times height equals the area. That matches the rectangle you built.

params.description: System fills all slots in sequence: area value in slot 1, fixed side 4 in slot 2, width and height in slots 3-4-5 matching the multiplication.

beats.text: The area is a multiple of 4 because when you multiply 4 times the number of rows, you get the area. That is what the sentence names. The area has to be a multiple of 4 because each row adds 4 more squares.

beats.text: Here's how this sentence works. The area is a multiple of 4 because 4 times the number of rows equals the area. The first blank names the area. The second blank names what it's a multiple of. The last three blanks show the multiplication that proves it.
